what_it_does: Defines a React `Badge` function component that renders a single element (default `<span>`) with a computed `cv-badge` className. It maps `tone` and `dot` props to BEM-style modifier classes (`cv-badge--<tone>`, `cv-badge--dot`), merges any caller `className`, passes remaining props through, and renders children inside.
observations: Uses `React.createElement` aliased to `h` rather than JSX, with a `/* global React */` comment implying React is a global rather than imported.
Header comment states tones map to system voices/states: gold (attention), success/warning/error (status), comm (sage relationship).
All visual styling is deferred to `.cv-badge` in `tokens/controls.css`; this file carries no styles.
Class list is assembled then `.filter(Boolean).join(' ')` to drop empty strings.
`as` prop allows rendering as a different element (default `span`); rest props are spread onto the element.
Exports both a named `Badge` and a default export of the same.
standouts: Tone tokens are described as 'voices/states' of a larger system, signalling a shared semantic vocabulary across components.
conventions: Follows the project's 'token-class wrapper' pattern: component is a thin shell, all visuals come from CSS tokens.
Matches the sibling Button/Card pattern (same `h = React.createElement`, same className-assembly idiom, same dual export).
notes: A ~20-line presentational label primitive: maps tone/dot props to cv-badge CSS classes; zero styling of its own.
questions: What is the full set of valid `tone` values the CSS actually defines (the comment lists some but not exhaustively)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header comment ('label primitive, token-class wrapper over .cv-badge') accurately describes the code.
apparent_themes: Semantic 'voice/state' vocabulary shared across the design system
intention_signals: Aims to give a typed, named badge primitive whose appearance is entirely governed by design tokens, so the same component recalibrates with the palette/theme without code change.
novelty: The framing of badge tones as system 'voices' (gold attention, comm sage relationship) rather than generic semantic colors.
contribution: Contributes the status/label primitive of this design system and its tone-to-voice mapping (attention/status/relationship), plus the polymorphic `as` + dot-modifier surface.
summary_for_embedding: A React Badge component that wraps the .cv-badge token class, mapping tone and dot props to CSS modifier classes for a token-styled label primitive.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a thin, theme-aware label/status badge primitive whose visuals are fully driven by design tokens.
